video_title: Nobody Wants This | Season 2 Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: The series is superb! Excellent overall in its sitcom reinvention, bringing it into the modern era, but it does have some flaws, including several clichés typical of American romantic relationships. It's all been done before. The girl and the guy really like each other, then want to take the next step and have sex, and then end up wondering if they're truly in love. And once again, it's the typical case of the girl who's searching for herself and wants to live and settle down with the perfect guy according to her criteria. The guy hesitates, etc... So, first and foremost, I want to say to impressionable young people, you have to be responsible before having sex and ask yourself the question well beforehand, not afterward, to know if it's really with the person you truly love. And once again, before moving in together, you have to think about it seriously and ask yourself the right questions. For that reason alone, I think it sends a bad message to young people, but well, that's just how Americans see it.

video_title: Nobody Wants This | Season 2 Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I loved the first season — it was sharp, funny, emotional, and felt real. But the second season? Honestly, it’s a disaster. The conversations are forced and unrealistic, the chemistry between the main characters is completely gone, and the whole story feels unnatural.

The main character acts like a child — she doesn’t communicate clearly, always wants to fight, and never takes responsibility. The sister is unbearable: loud, negative, intrusive, and fake. Forcing her sister to talk about her private life on a podcast is just absurd. In real life, that relationship would’ve ended a long time ago.

The parents are confusing too — the mother seems lost, the father behaves like a teenager, and the brother’s storyline makes no sense. Nothing feels authentic anymore.

I kept watching just to see how it ends, but honestly, it’s cringe from start to finish. Season 2 completely lost the charm, the realism, and the emotional connection that made Season 1 so good.
